panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth stands in a sunlit parlor talking to her aunt, her expression thoughtful and earnest. Soft Victorian-era interiors, detailed period clothing, warm lighting.

bubbles.text: I will remain patient, Aunt. I shall not burden Mother with my suspicions.

panels.description: Wide shot: Mr. Collins arrives at the Longbourn estate in a carriage, looking pompous. Mrs. Bennet watches from the doorway with a look of weary resignation.

bubbles.text: Mr. Collins returned to Hertfordshire, and Mrs. Bennet accepted the inevitable.

panels.description: Medium close-up: Charlotte Lucas and Elizabeth sitting on a sofa. Elizabeth looks down in shame, biting her lip, while Charlotte observes her with sympathetic concern.

bubbles.text: I am so embarrassed by her conduct, Charlotte.

panels.description: Medium shot: Elizabeth looking out a window at the English countryside, her expression hesitant but resolute, holding a travel cloak.

bubbles.text: I shall visit you in Hunsford. It is settled.

panels.description: Close up: Elizabeth's hands holding a letter, her eyes scanning the ink with intense interest and eagerness. Background softly blurred to focus on the parchment.

bubbles.text: The first letter arrived. Within its lines lay the promise of Hunsford and news of Lady Catherine.
